2605.13429.title: TokAlign++: Advancing Vocabulary Adaptation via Better Token Alignment
2605.13429.paper_summary: Tokenization is a foundational step in the text process of Large Language Models (LLMs). Texts must be first tokenized into token IDs, which are then input to LLMs. Inefficient tokenization results in long token-ID sequences and will slow down the training and inference of LLMs. The fine-grained knowledge transfer between LLMs, like token-level distillation, is also impeded by the mismatch in vocabulary. To bridge this gap, we introduce a method named TokAlign++ to improve vocabulary adaptation performance by learning better token alignment lexicon. The source and target vocabularies are taken as two different languages, and the bilingual token alignment lexicon is learned from monolingual token representations. Model parameters are rearranged following this bilingual lexicon for new vocabulary, and progressively fine-tuned for adaptation. Experimental results on 15 languages show that our method boosts the multilingual text compression rates and preserves most of the multilingual ability of vanilla models. It costs as few as 1k steps to restore the performance of the vanilla model. After unifying vocabularies between vanilla models, token-level distillation remarkably improves the base model with only 235M tokens.
2605.13429.paper_summary_zh: 標記化是大型語言模型（LLMs）文本處理中的基礎步驟。文本必須首先被標記化為標記 ID，然後再輸入到 LLMs 中。低效的標記化會導致長的標記 ID 序列，並會減慢 LLMs 的訓練和推理。LLMs 之間的細粒度知識轉移，例如標記級蒸餾，也受到詞彙不匹配的阻礙。為了彌補這一差距，我們提出了一種名為 TokAlign++ 的方法，通過學習更好的標記對齊詞彙來改善詞彙適應性能。源詞彙和目標詞彙被視為兩種不同的語言，雙語標記對齊詞彙是從單語標記表示中學習的。模型參數根據這個雙語詞彙進行重新排列，以適應新的詞彙，並逐步進行微調以適應。對 15 種語言的實驗結果顯示，我們的方法提高了多語言文本壓縮率，並保留了大多數原始模型的多語言能力。恢復原始模型性能僅需 1k 步。統一原始模型之間的詞彙後，標記級蒸餾顯著改善了基礎模型，僅需 235M 標記。
2605.13429.author: Chong Li et.al.
2605.13429.authors: Chong Li, Yingzhuo Deng, Wen Yang, Jiajun Zhang, Chengqing Zong
